title: [Pipeline] Plumb values through the pipeline + Support multi-cycle Ops
body:
The `pipeline` dialect is a WIP when it comes to handling multi-cycle operations within the pipeline. Neither does it explicitly plumb the values to its user Op. Finally, the pipeline does not model memory resource constraints on loads and stores, which is the most common reason for needing to plumb values into deeper stages.

This pass first registers the scheduling problem with memory resource constraints, then it plumbs the values as needed through the pipeline stages. Since multi-cycle ops cannot have their results registered within the stage that they start, the pass is now aware of which stages to register the result in and value plumbing is no longer implicit.

Future TODOs:
- ~~Fix failure on `@minimal` in `loops.mlir` regression tests.~~
- Add new test cases with multi-cycle Ops
- Refine how the `ModuloProblem` is constructed w.r.t. resource constraints (right now I duplicate the `CyclicProblem`)
- ~~Need scheduling to work #4375~~
- ~~Remove any redundant data structures~~
- Consider if operator info should be preserved in output
- Handle II limits from iteration argument hazards
  - Handle case where iteration arguments need to be plumbed an extra distance when Iter arg recurrence II < min (`memref` recurrence II, resource II)
  - ~~Handle case where iteration argument use dependence is the II bottleneck~~
